label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the text carefully to find out how long the experiment lasted. The question is asking about the duration of the experiment. The options are A) about three days, B) about ten days, C) about two weeks, D) about a month. First, let's look for any mention of time in the text. The third paragraph says: "They were assigned to write for 20 minutes per day for three consecutive days." So the participants wrote for three days. But this is only the writing part of the experiment, not the entire experiment. We need to check if the experiment ended there or if it continued beyond that. Then, in the fourth paragraph: "Two weeks after the third day of writing, all participants received a standard 4mm skin biopsy..." So two weeks after the writing sessions, the biopsy was done. The wound healing was monitored after that. In the fifth paragraph: "On the 11th day after the biopsy, the wounds completely healed..." So 11 days after the biopsy, which was two weeks (14 days) after the writing sessions. So the timeline is: - Participants wrote for three days. - Two weeks (14 days) after the third day of writing, the biopsy was performed. - The healing was monitored over the following days. - The results were observed on the 11th day after the biopsy. Therefore, the total duration of the experiment from the start of the writing sessions to the 11th day after the biopsy would be: - 3 days (writing) + 14 days (before biopsy) + 11 days (after biopsy) = 28 days. But does the question refer to the entire duration of the experiment? The question asks "the experiment lasted". The experiment included the writing
